design_philosophy.core_loop: Zelda-like action/adventure loop: explore areas, defeat enemies, collect items and equipment, advance through story stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat against enemies and bosses using equipped weapons; navigation through obstacle-filled environments
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Robin Hood performing acts of heroism and thievery, progressing through story moments from the 1991 film
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed film adaptation as action/adventure (not pure platformer mechanics); segmented stage-based progression with password system indicating discrete checkpoints rather than seamless exploration; inventory management with weight/capacity system adds resource tension

difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based system allowing skip to any major story segment (CATACOMB, WALL, LOCKSLEY, DUBOIS, CHASE, CATHEDRA, BOAR, MASTER, CHAPEL, WELL, TAX, POND, VILLAGE, CELTS, TOWN, TOWNHANG, CASTLEIN, WEDDING); passwords provide only items strictly needed for that area
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency via password system; players can skip content, retry specific segments, or attempt full run. Inventory weight system creates resource management challenge

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon and armor upgrades with incremental stat improvements (Knifeâ†’Swordâ†’Short Swordâ†’Steel Swordâ†’Claymore hierarchy; similar progressions for armor and bows). Items have attack and weight values affecting combat capability and carrying capacity
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated: defeat bosses and complete objectives to progress through narrative segments (Arab jailâ†’Hadrian's Wallâ†’Locksley Castleâ†’Dubois Manorâ†’Horse raceâ†’Cathedral rescue, etc.)
replayability_hooks: Password system enables quick access to any stage for challenge attempts
Multiple weapon/armor variants with different stat profiles encourage experimenting with loadouts
Unused items in code (Shield, Locksley Shield, Ladder) suggest developer intended more customization depth

world_building_techniques: Film license provides external narrative context (1991 Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves movie)
Stage names and progression directly map to story beats (CHAPEL=Meet Marian, WEDDING=End)
Location-specific palettes and sprites (pal.sherwood, pal.loxley.dark, pal.duboisout) create environmental distinction
Named NPCs in code (sprite.face.tuck.chr, sprite.face.guard.chr, sprite.will.chr) indicate character-driven narrative

boss_design.philosophy: Duel-based combat encounters integrated into narrative progression; debug menu explicitly lists 'fight any duel in the game' suggesting duels as primary boss mechanism
notable_bosses: Implicit antagonists at story junctures (Dubois, horse chase sequence)

faction_indicators: Sheriff's forces (guards, castle soldiers implied by CASTLEIN, CASTLEDO sprites)
danger_signals: Gallows location (TOWNHANG=In town, by gallows) signals execution threat
Sheriff's castle as final confrontation space
Horse chase sequence implies pursuit/escape danger
Gold wagon attacks (TAX stage) involve enemy patrols
environmental_storytelling: Haunted well location (WELL=Search for haunted well) suggests supernatural/mystery elements
Healing water source (POND stage) implies resource scarcity
Locksley Castle and Dubois Manor as distinct faction strongholds
Church/Cathedral as sanctuary space (CHAPEL meeting point)
